info.name: Pawffy API — Unified
info.description:
Complete Pawffy backend API collection — `{{baseUrl}}`

## What this collection includes
- Customer / core backend APIs
- Admin-protected endpoints
- Vendor onboarding, live vendor app, account/security, and adoption flows
- Public discovery, support, and static-content endpoints

## Recommended usage
1. Use `baseUrl` for your current environment. Default is local: `{{localBaseUrl}}`.
2. Start with `🔐 Auth` for customer/general flows or `Vendor Auth` for partner flows.
3. Use the saved variables (`token`, `adminToken`, IDs, OTP/token helpers) as requests populate them.
4. For production testing, switch `baseUrl` to `{{productionBaseUrl}}` and follow the production notes in the vendor account/security requests.

## Folder layout
- Core app folders come from the general backend collection.
- Vendor-specific folders are appended after the core folders so all APIs live in one importable file.

## Security and behavior notes
- Protected routes require `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` unless they specifically use `adminToken`.
- JWT sessions use `tokenVersion`, so logout/password change/contact verification can invalidate older tokens.
- Wallet payment routes only work when `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true`.
- In non-production, some vendor verification flows may return OTP/token helpers for local testing; production does not return those secrets.

## Important variables
baseUrl · localBaseUrl · productionBaseUrl · token · adminToken · userId · petId · vetId · serviceId · bookingId · businessId · requestId · adoptionId · conversationId · receiverId · emailOtp · phoneOtp · adoptionPaymentIntentId

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Rate limit: 10 req / 15 min on register & login · 5 req / hour on forgot-password
Logout increments tokenVersion → all issued tokens become invalid instantly

request.description:
⚠️ Rate limited: 10 attempts / 15 min

Password policy: min 8 chars, must contain uppercase + lowercase + digit
Example valid: Password123

⚠️ Rate limited: 10 attempts / 15 min
Failed attempts are logged with IP.

exec: if (!r.data?.token) console.warn('No token returned — check OTP / environment');

request.description: Completes login using the SMS OTP. In production, paste the OTP into `phoneOtp` first.
Step 1 (password) for phone-login. Saves `phoneOtp` in non-prod if OTP delivery is not configured.

exec: if (!r.data?.token) console.warn('No token returned — check OTP / environment');

request.description: Completes login using the phone OTP. In production, paste the SMS OTP into `phoneOtp` first.

exec: console.log('Token cleared. All sessions on all devices are now invalid.');

request.description:
Increments tokenVersion in DB → every JWT issued before this call is instantly rejected.
All devices are logged out simultaneously.

⚠️ Rate limited: 5 req / hour

Dev mode: resetToken returned in response.data.resetToken
Production with SMTP: email sent only, token NOT in response.

Always returns 200 (prevents email enumeration).

Token expires in 1 hour. Single use only.
Also increments tokenVersion → logs out all devices.

Rate limit: 5 requests/hour per IP (same as forgot-password).

exec: console.log('✅ New token saved — other sessions logged out');

request.description: Logs out all other devices. Returns a fresh token for the current session.

item.name: Get Vets — Search by name / clinic

request.description:
🔒 Requires role: admin or partner

Partners may only update the vet profile linked to their account email.
Rating cannot be set here — it is computed from verified reviews only.
Partners may only manage availability for their own vet profile (email match).

Returns all time slots with available:true/false.
Slots already booked (pending/confirmed) are marked available:false.
Returns name, description, price, duration for each active service.

🔒 Admin/Partner only. Create one service per request.
Common services to seed:
• General Consultancy $40
• Vaccination $60
• Emergency Care $100
• Dental Care $70
• Others $70

Partners may only manage services for their own vet profile (email match).

rating: 1–5 (integer). bookingId is required.
User must have a completed booking with this vet.
One review per user per vet.
Average rating on Vet is auto-recalculated.

item.name: 📅 Bookings — Full Flow
item.description:
Complete booking flow:
1. GET /vets/:vetId/services  → select service + price
2. GET /vets/:vetId/slots?date → pick time slot
3. GET /pets                    → pick pet
4. Client collects symptoms/reason
5. POST /bookings               → create booking
6. GET /payments/summary/:id   → price breakdown
7. POST /payments/apply-coupon → optional coupon
8a. POST /payments/create-intent → card/net_banking (Stripe)
8b. POST /payments/confirm       → wallet (disabled unless WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true)
8c. POST /payments/verify        → sync Stripe after payment sheet
9. GET /bookings/:id            → confirmation screen

Status: customers can PATCH cancelled only; admins can set any status.

body.raw:
{
  "petId": "{{petId}}",
  "vetId": "{{vetId}}",
  "serviceId": "{{serviceId}}",
  "bookingType": "veterinarian",
  "bookingDate": "2026-07-15",
  "bookingTime": "11:00",
  "reasonForVisit": "My dog has been lethargic for 2 days",
  "symptoms": "Vomiting,Lethargy,Loss of appetite",
  "symptomsDuration": "2 Days ago",
  "notes": "Please check ears as well"
}

request.description:
bookingType: veterinarian | grooming | walking | training | boarding | sitting | sitter | poop_scooper | transport

symptomsDuration: Today | 2 Days ago | A week ago | More than a week

Conflict check: returns 409 if that slot is already booked.

Returns: servicePrice + platformFee($5) + tax(5%) - discount = total
Also returns pawPoints earned.

item.name: STEP 8a — Create Stripe PaymentIntent (Card / Net Banking)

request.description:
paymentMethod: card | net_banking

Returns clientSecret → pass to flutter_stripe:
  Stripe.instance.initPaymentSheet(paymentIntentClientSecret: clientSecret)
  Stripe.instance.presentPaymentSheet()

Booking confirmed automatically via Stripe webhook.

Wallet payment — no Stripe.

⚠️ Disabled by default (503) unless server env WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true and wallet balance logic is implemented.
Use STEP 8a (Stripe) for production payments.

Call after flutter_stripe payment sheet closes.
Syncs Stripe status with DB if webhook hasn't fired yet.
Returns: stripeStatus, paymentStatus, bookingStatus, appointmentId
Returns: appointmentId, vet, pet, dateTimeFormatted, totalPaid, paymentStatus
🔒 Requires role: admin

Customers (booking owner) may only set status to "cancelled".
Admins may set: pending | confirmed | completed | cancelled.
Confirmation/completion for customers happens via payment flow or admin action.

item.name: 🏠 Dashboard
item.description: Optional Bearer on POST /dashboard for personalized data. User/notifications endpoints require self or admin.

request.description: Send Bearer token for user profile + notifications. Without token: public data only (categories, partners, banner, quote). Do NOT send userId in body.
🔒 Requires auth. Returns private profile only for self or admin.
🌐 Public. Returns partners within 25km (no email/phone exposed).
🔒 Requires auth. Own notifications or admin only.

item.name: 🚨 Lost Pets
item.description:
Owner flow — report **your own** missing pet.

Required: pet photo(s), name, color, breed, gender, description, last-seen location.
Optional: age, height, weight.

Upload images as files (`images` field, max 3). JSON body with image URLs is still supported for testing.

formdata.description: Pet photo (JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 2MB). Add up to 3 files with the same field name.

formdata.value: Last seen near park wearing red collar

request.description: Owner reports their missing pet. Upload 1–3 photos using field name `images`. Age/height/weight are optional.

item.name: 🔍 Found Pets
item.description:
Stranger flow — report a pet you **saw/found** but do not own.

Required: photo(s), color, description, location.
Optional: breed, gender (defaults to `Unknown` if omitted).

You do NOT need to know the pet's name, exact breed, age, height, or weight.

formdata.description: Photo of the pet you saw (JPEG/PNG/WebP, max 2MB)

formdata.value: Medium-sized friendly dog near the station. Looked lost, no collar visible.

request.description: For people who spotted a stray/lost pet. Breed and gender are optional.
Returns lostPets and foundPets arrays combined.

item.name: 🚶 Walking Bookings
item.description:
partnerId must be a user with role=partner.
Pets in selectedPetList must belong to the authenticated user.
paymentStatus is server-controlled.

request.description:
partnerId must be a user with role=partner.
selectedPetList must contain pet IDs owned by the authenticated user.
paymentStatus is set server-side (always Pending) — do not send it in the body.

item.name: Vendor Auth
item.description: Vendor-specific authentication for partner accounts. Use `POST /api/auth/vendor/register` and `POST /api/auth/vendor/login` here; admin login remains in the vendor admin folder.

request.description: Creates partner user + draft business profile. Requires `acceptTerms: true`.
Partner accounts only. Returns 403 for customer/admin accounts.

exec: if (!r.data?.token) console.warn('No token returned — check OTP / environment');

request.description: Completes vendor login using the SMS OTP. In production, paste the OTP into `phoneOtp` first.

item.name: 2. Onboarding (before approval)
item.description:
Use only while `verificationStatus` is `incomplete` or `rejected`.
Blocked once status is `pending` or `verified`.

2. Run Login Admin → saves `adminToken`
3. List Pending → saves `businessId`
4. Approve Application

item.name: 8.5 Account & Security
item.description:
Phase 2 vendor account-security flows.

Non-production: the API may return `phoneOtp` / `emailOtp` and the collection scripts will save them automatically.

Production: the API will not return those secrets. Use the OTP received over SMS and the email OTP delivered to the new email address, then paste the real value into the corresponding collection variable before running the verify request.

request.description: Starts a secure phone update. In production, expect the OTP to be delivered outside the API response.
Completes phone verification using the real OTP. In production, paste the SMS OTP into `phoneOtp` first.
Starts a secure email change. In non-production the API may return `otp`. In production the OTP is delivered by Twilio SendGrid (or SMTP fallback) and is not returned.
Completes the email swap using the 6-digit OTP. In production, paste the OTP from the email into `emailOtp` first.

item.name: 8.6 Adoptions
item.description: Vendor adoption workflow for pet-based applications. Use the wallet payment request only when wallet payments are enabled in the backend; otherwise use the Stripe intent + verify sequence.

request.description: Upload one document at a time using field name `document`. Supported types mirror other vendor document uploads.
Use only when `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true` in the backend environment.
Run after Stripe confirms the intent succeeded; this finalizes the adoption status in the backend.
